raw_text:

{
  "claims": [
    "Tribal court judges currently do not receive the same legal protections as state and federal judges.",
    "AB 2281 aims to provide tribal court judges with legal protections comparable to those of state and federal judges.",
    "California Indian Legal Services has collaborated with many tribes to develop their judicial systems, which is essential for their sovereignty.",
    "AB 2281 seeks to address the legal disparity that doesn't grant tribal court judges the same protections as other judicial officers.",
    "AB 2281 allows a tribal court judge of a federally recognized California Indian Tribe to have their residence address, telephone number, and email address confidential on voter registration forms under specific conditions.",
    "AB 2281 permits a tribal judge to request the Department of Motor Vehicles to keep their home address confidential.",
    "AB 2281 makes it punishable by imprisonment to assault a judge or former judge of a tribal court in retaliation for their official duties."
  ]
